In init, we have candidate_prepared shift_candidate_alpha, candidate_task_profile_linked shift_candidate_alpha route_task_profile_alpha, operational_constraint_available operational_constraint_alpha, candidate_operational_constraint_linked shift_candidate_alpha operational_constraint_alpha. Next, operator_alignment_confirmed requires constraint_applied and candidate_operator_linked. We have candidate_operator_linked shift_candidate_alpha driver_operator_alpha. So after constraint_applied, we can confirm operator alignment via action confirm_operator_alignment (parameters ?candidate ?operator_resource). Then compliance_validation_complete requires operator_alignment_confirmed and candidate_compliance_certificate_linked. But we don't have that link yet. So we need to assign compliance certificate. Also we need candidate_compliance_certificate_linked as part of goal. So we need to assign compliance_certificate_alpha to shift_candidate_alpha. Action assign_compliance_certificate requires candidate_prepared (true) and compliance_certificate_available (true). So we can do that.

Then we need compliance_validation_complete. Action verify_compliance_certificate requires operator_alignment_confirmed and candidate_compliance_certificate_linked. So after assigning and confirming alignment, we can do that.

Then we need dispatch_ready. That can be achieved by finalizing candidate. There are multiple ways to finalize: finalize_candidate_after_compliance_validation, finalize_candidate_after_sequence_validation, finalize_candidate_direct. The simplest after compliance validation is to use finalize_candidate_after_compliance_validation. Its precondition: (compliance_validation_complete ?candidate) (not (candidate_finalized ?candidate)). Effect: (candidate_finalized ?candidate) (dispatch_ready ?candidate). So that will also give dispatch_ready. And candidate_finalized is also goal.

Now we also need operator_available driver_operator_alpha. To achieve that, we need to release operator from shift_candidate_alpha.
